ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Visit name
Notes: e.g., Baseline, 6-Month Follow Up, Year 2, Daily

ElementDescription: Please indicate your current level of stress as it pertains to each description. Tense
Notes: 1 =  Definitely describes your feelings; 2 = More or less describes your feelings; 3 =  Cannot decide whether or not it describes how you feel; 4 = Does not describe the way you feel

ElementDescription: Stress Score
Notes: Sum of sac_1s, sac_2s, sac_3s, sac_5s, sac_6s, sac_9s, sac_10s, sac_11s, sac_12s, sac_13s, sac_15s, sac_18s, sac_21s, sac_22s, sac_23s, sac_25s, sac_27s, sac_28s. Mackay et al 1978 criteria. All scores converted to 0 or 1 and summed.

ElementDescription: Arousal Score
Notes: Sum of sac_4a, sac_7a, sac_8a, sac_14a, sac_16a, sac_17a, sac_19a, sac_20a, sac_24a, sac_26a, sac_29a, sac_30a. Mackay et al 1978 criteria. All scores converted to 0 or 1 and summed.
